name_en: Lunch — Wondong Minari Samgyeopsal (plum season) or Gyeongju

en_fields.description: Lunch is at one of two coach-friendly options depending on the season and routing: (1) **Wondong-myeon Minari (미나리) Samgyeopsal** — Wondong township is famous across Korea for **dropwort (Korean water-celery / minari) farms**, peaking from **February to April**. The signature dish: thick-cut samgyeopsal pork belly grilled tableside and wrapped in fresh raw minari (~₩15,000-20,000 per person, traditionally served with soju). The minari season conveniently overlaps with Tongdosa's plum-blossom season — a natural seasonal pairing that locals know but few foreign tourists discover. Or (2) **Gyeongju local restaurant** if the day is running behind schedule or if minari is out of season — Gyeongju ssambap (Korean wrap-and-eat set meal) ~₩13,000-18,000 per person, or the heritage **Gyodong Beopju** rice wine + traditional set menu at Yosukgung 1779 in Gyochon Hanok Village (~₩30,000-50,000 set, premium option). Lunch is at the guest's own expense; vegetarian + halal-friendly options are limited at samgyeopsal restaurants — please notify the guide at booking if dietary accommodations are needed and a substitute (typically a bibimbap or hansik buffet) will be arranged.
en_fields.whyOnRoute: Lunch is timed at 11:15 immediately after Tongdosa to bridge the morning's Yangsan cluster and the afternoon's Gyeongju cluster (Bulguksa + Bomun Lake). The Wondong minari samgyeopsal option is intentional during plum/cherry season because Wondong-myeon (~10 min from Tongdosa) is on the natural coach route toward Gyeongju and leverages a regional seasonal specialty that pairs the day's plum-cherry theme with an authentic plate-level Korean farm-to-table experience that's very rare in international tour itineraries.
highlights: **Wondong Minari Samgyeopsal** — dropwort + pork belly seasonal pairing (Feb-Apr peak)
Minari season conveniently overlaps Tongdosa's Jajangmae plum-blossom season

Lunch own expense; vegetarian / halal substitution available on request

en_fields.description: **Pickup is consolidated across the four standard Jeju City pickup points** to keep the morning compact and the eastbound run-out clean: **Jeju International Airport (제주공항)** for guests with same-day arrivals or airport-area hotels (Hidden Cliff, Maison Glad, Lotte City, etc.); **Jeju City Hall (제주시청)** for the Yeon-dong / Nohyeong / Old Town hotel cluster; **Hagwi-dong (하귀동)** as the westernmost Jeju City pickup; and **Dongmun Traditional Market (동문시장)** for the eastern Jeju City Tapdong/Sarabong waterfront cluster. Sequence runs **Hagwi → Airport → City Hall → Dongmun → eastbound coastal route 1132** toward Hamdeok. The minicoach is **small-group (max ~14 pax)** with a licensed English-speaking guide. **Bilingual printed itinerary card** is distributed at first pickup, and the guide gives a weather brief plus a **hydrangea bloom-status read** (which gardens are at peak vs. early bloom that morning) so the photo-stop priorities can be calibrated for the day.
en_fields.whyOnRoute: **Operational anchor for the eastern hydrangea loop.** Pickup is the only point in the day where four scattered hotel zones converge — once east of Dongmun, the minicoach commits to the coastal Route 1132 corridor that defines the day (Hamdeok → Seongeup → Seongsan → Ilchul Land → Hueree). The west-to-east pickup ordering is intentional: it lets the coach pick up Hagwi guests first and use the remaining 25 minutes after Dongmun to reach Hamdeok in time for the morning beach tide window before busloads arrive.
highlights: **4 pickup points** — Airport, City Hall, Hagwi, Dongmun (Tapdong)
Sequence west→east minimizes morning coach distance
**Small-group minicoach (max 14 pax)** with licensed English guide
Bilingual itinerary card distributed at first pickup
**Hydrangea bloom-status brief** at first stop (peak vs. early-bloom calibration)
Total morning transit Jeju City → Hamdeok Beach: ~25 min

category: Midday Reset — South-West Jeju Local Cuisine

en_fields.description: **60-minute reset between Jusangjeolli and Songaksan** — Daejeong / Andeok corridor sits 10–15 min from both stops. Default rotation: **galchi-jorim (갈치조림, braised cutlassfish)** — Jeju's south coast is Korea's primary cutlassfish landing, with Daejeong harbor delivering daily catches; **mulhoe (물회)** in summer; **heuk-doeji gui (흑돼지구이, Jeju black pig BBQ)** year-round (Cultural Heritage Material No. 5, 2015); or — **west-south specialty** — **gosari yukgaejang (고사리 육개장, fern + beef spicy soup)** rooted in Jeju's mountain-fern foraging tradition, especially common in Daejeong-eup. **Vegetarian + halal-friendly options** available at one venue in the rotation — disclose at pickup. Lunch venue is **10-min coach to Songaksan** trailhead.
en_fields.whyOnRoute: Daejeong-eup positioning = 10-min hop to Songaksan. **Galchi from Daejeong harbor + gosari yukgaejang** are both regional specialties anchored in the Daejeong / Andeok tradition (Daejeong was the historical Jeong-eui-hyeon administrative seat for west-south Jeju, similar to Seongeup's role in east Jeju). The fern-foraging tradition ties to Jeju's **mountain-folk food heritage**, distinct from the coastal seafood emphasis of east Jeju. Pace also breaks the morning's alpine + Geopark walking before the afternoon's Songaksan loop + waterfall + tea museum + beach sunset.
highlights: **West-south Jeju seafood + mountain-cuisine corridor** — Daejeong / Andeok
**Galchi-jorim (갈치조림)** — Daejeong harbor primary landing
**Gosari yukgaejang (고사리 육개장)** — Jeju mountain-fern + beef spicy soup, west-south specialty
**Heuk-doeji** year-round — Cultural Heritage Material No. 5 (2015)
**Vegetarian + halal-friendly** options on request
**10-min coach to Songaksan** — minimal post-lunch transit

en_fields.description: Pickup is consolidated at three central Busan transit hubs to keep the day manageable without crisscrossing the city: **Busan Station (Line 1)** for guests staying near the KTX terminal or in the Choryang/Nampo-dong area; **Seomyeon Station (Lines 1 & 2)** — Busan's busiest subway interchange, central for guests in mid-city hotels around Lotte Hotel Busan or the Seomyeon Medical Street; and **Haeundae Station (Line 2)** for guests in the beachfront resort district near Westin Josun, Park Hyatt, or Signiel Busan. The coach picks up at all three in sequence (typically Busan Station first ~09:00, Seomyeon ~09:15, Haeundae ~09:30) and then heads northeast along the coastal Gwangan Bridge route toward Gijang-gun for the day's first stop. Total transit from final pickup to Haedong Yonggungsa: ~30-40 minutes depending on traffic. Driver will share a printed Korean-English itinerary card with timings; please be at your pickup point 5 minutes before the scheduled time to keep the day on track for the later UN Memorial Cemetery and Gamcheon stops, which have stricter operating windows.
en_fields.whyOnRoute: Three pickup points cover Busan's three main hotel zones (KTX/Nampo, Seomyeon mid-city, Haeundae beachfront) without forcing guests to transit across the city in the morning. Sequencing northwest→east minimizes coach distance and lands the group at Haedong Yonggungsa around 10:00 — early enough to beat the busiest tour-bus arrivals.
highlights: Three pickup options across central Busan (Busan Station, Seomyeon, Haeundae)
Coach is small-group (max ~14 pax)
Total transit to first stop: ~30-40 min
Printed Korean-English itinerary card distributed at pickup

en_fields.description: **60-minute reset between Camellia Hill and Cheonjeyeon Falls** — the south Jeju coast is one of Korea's premier seafood landing zones, and the Andeok-Seogwipo corridor sits 10–15 min from the next stop. Default rotation: **galchi-jorim (갈치조림, braised cutlassfish)** — Jeju is Korea's primary cutlassfish landing, with the south coast delivering the freshest catches; **mulhoe (물회, cold raw-fish soup)** in summer (June–September); or **heuk-doeji gui (흑돼지구이, Jeju black pig BBQ)** year-round (heritage breed designated **Cultural Heritage Material No. 5, 2015**). **Vegetarian alternative**: bibim-guksu with seasonal Jeju vegetables. **Halal-friendly options** available at one venue in the rotation — disclose at pickup. Lunch venue is **10-min coach to Cheonjeyeon Falls** entrance.
en_fields.whyOnRoute: South-coast positioning means **10-min hop to Cheonjeyeon Falls** afterward — preserves photo-window timing. Galchi is also a south-Jeju regional specialty (Jeju is Korea's primary cutlassfish landing port), so the geographic placement adds culinary identity to the lunch slot. Pace also breaks the morning's Hallasan + botanical garden walking before the afternoon's three remaining UNESCO/heritage anchors.

Typical menu options include **abalone stone-pot rice (전복돌솥밥)** — Jeju's signature dish leveraging the island's haenyeo-harvested abalone — or **black pork samgyeopsal** (Jeju's heritage breed of black pig, designated Cultural Heritage Material No. 5 in 2015). Vegetarian option available on request. Cost ₩15,000–₩22,000 pp, NOT included in tour price. The 60-min window is timed to land lunch right after the Noksan-ro drive — guests are typically hungry from the cherry/canola photo session and ready for a warm meal.
en_fields.whyOnRoute: Lunch lands at the geographic midpoint between morning cherry drive and afternoon Ilchul Land + Seongsan Ilchulbong segment — minimizes backtracking and gives guests a warm-meal break between two outdoor sessions.

**Same-day evening flights from JIA** typically viable (last departure ~21:30)
Drop-off sequence aligned with morning pickup mirroring

en_fields.description: Private vehicle returns directly to your departure point — **Busan Port International Cruise Terminal** in Dongsam-dong (Yeongdo) for large-ship cruises, or **Busan Port International Passenger Terminal** in Choryang for medium cruises and ferries — buffered to **deliver you 60–90 minutes before your ship's all-aboard time**. Driver coordinates with your morning departure point; if your itinerary changed mid-day (e.g., late lunch), the driver alerts dispatch to adjust the buffer. Drop-off is at the terminal arrival hall, where the cruise line's own gangway shuttle typically operates.
en_fields.whyOnRoute: Direct return without intermediate stops protects the all-aboard buffer. 30 minutes covers worst-case rush-hour transit from Nampo-dong back to either terminal; lighter traffic = earlier delivery (drop-off can be optimized for your preferred arrival time).
highlights: Direct return to original cruise terminal (Dongsam or Choryang)
**60–90 min buffer before all-aboard**
Real-time dispatch coordination if mid-day timing shifts

en_fields.description: Small-group van returns to the **same cruise terminal where the tour began** — Busan Port International Cruise Terminal (Dongsam) for large-ship cruises, or Busan Port International Passenger Terminal (Choryang) for medium cruises — buffered to deliver passengers **60–90 minutes before all-aboard**. Drop-off is at the terminal arrival hall, where the cruise line's gangway shuttle typically operates. Group does not detour for individual hotel drop-offs (use the private charter alternative if hotel return is needed).
en_fields.whyOnRoute: Direct return without intermediate stops protects the all-aboard buffer for all passengers simultaneously. 30 minutes covers worst-case rush-hour transit from Nampo-dong back to either terminal.
highlights: Direct group return to original cruise terminal
**60–90 min buffer before all-aboard**
**No individual hotel drop-offs** (small-group fixed schedule)
